what_it_does: The agent-skill entrypoint for the ConceptV Design System. Carries YAML frontmatter (name: conceptv-design, user-invocable) and a description telling agents to use it to generate well-branded ConceptV interfaces/assets. The body is a prioritised read-list of the most important files to load, a condensed statement that the system is generative (v2), the product surfaces and their shapes, concrete on-brand design rules (type/colour/texture/motion/signature-moves/avoid), how to use the skill for visual-artifact vs production-code requests, icon-library usage, and flagged open questions (substituted fonts, offline website).
observations: Frontmatter declares it a user-invocable skill named conceptv-design for production or throwaway prototypes.
Gives a numbered load-order of 10 file groups (README, colors_and_type.css, logos, icons, illustrations, ui_kits, preview, DESIGN-LANGUAGE.md, analysis docs, core/templates).
Restates the v2 thesis: one type system + one rule engine + two layout solvers via axis-dials, design = f(content, axisPosition), 'v2 wins' on conflict.
Instructs 'Don't hand-lay-out a layout' — use window.ConceptVDesignSystem_c8f43c's Slide/RenderType with an archetype and content.
Lists the colour ramp tokens --ramp-1..4 (#dad364 -> #d6bf57 -> #c09d5d -> #b98664) and distinguishes the true logo gold #E0C010 from the softer applied deck gold.
States colour-role logic: ink=content, gold=active/decision/selected (never default background), bronze=structural/quiet voice.
Enumerates six signature moves and an explicit 'Avoid' list (pure-white backgrounds, cool greys, ALL-CAPS, emoji in body, Lucide/Feather in diagrams).
Gives three concrete icon-usage code snippets (React CvIcon, static SVG img, vanilla CV_ICONS).
Tells the agent, if invoked without guidance, to ask audience/surface/fidelity/variation-count then act as expert designer.
standouts: Operational read-order that ranks which files to load first for an agent (a load contract, not just a list).
The crisp colour-role logic line (ink=content, gold=decision, bronze=quiet voice) as a single rule.
The 'Avoid' list including the specific instruction not to replace the bronze icon library with Lucide/Feather in diagrams.
conventions: Standard Claude-skill format: YAML frontmatter (name/description/user-invocable) + markdown body.
Defers to README/DESIGN-LANGUAGE/analysis as canonical and positions itself as the condensed entry pointer.
Uses fenced jsx/html code blocks for usage examples.
Mirrors README's voice rules and v2 precedence ('v2 wins').
notes: The skill an agent invokes to design on-brand for ConceptV — read it as a condensed, action-oriented index that points into the README/analysis and gives the do/avoid rules and icon snippets.
questions: Are the exact ramp hex values here kept in sync with colors_and_type.css, or could they drift as a second copy?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: frontmatter promises a skill to generate well-branded ConceptV interfaces and the body delivers the load-order, rules, and usage guidance an agent needs to do that.
apparent_themes: On-brand discipline via explicit do/avoid rules
intention_signals: Trying to give an agent the minimum it must load and the rules it must follow to produce on-brand ConceptV artifacts, while routing it to the deeper canonical docs for everything else.
contribution: Contributes the actionable, condensed designer brief: the file load-order, the colour-role logic, the signature-moves/avoid lists, and ready-to-paste icon usage — the operational distillation of the brand for an agent doing a build.
summary_for_embedding: ConceptV Design System skill entrypoint (frontmatter + body) giving agents a prioritised file load-order, condensed v2 generative model, on-brand colour/type/motion rules, do/avoid lists, and icon-library usage for generating branded interfaces.
intention_for_embedding: To let an agent invoke a skill that quickly equips it to design well-branded ConceptV interfaces and prototypes by loading the right files and applying the brand rules.
